title: hibbertia fractiflexa subsp. serotina
intro: Hibbertia fractiflexa subsp. serotina, also known as the yellow daisy or Guinea flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. Hibbertia fractiflexa subsp. serotina can be prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal purposes, although its effectiveness and safety should be evaluated by a healthcare professional.
